c74object.name: tap.808.clap~
c74object.digest: TR-808-style handclap/maracas voice, synthesized from the analog circuit
c74object.description:
The tap.808.clap~ object is a circuit-informed model of the Roland TR-808's CP/MA voice channel — one circuit the hardware switches between two sounds, here the model attribute. The clap is band-passed noise (~2 kHz, straight from the schematic's filter values) driven by the hardware's celebrated three-teeth sawtooth envelope — three rapid bursts ~10 ms apart, the "multiple hands" transient — plus a parallel longer decay, the clap's reverberation wash. The maracas model is the same channel's short, bright high-passed noise burst. Trigger with a signal: a rising edge fires the voice, and the edge's amplitude (0..1) is the accent. Single-channel: wrap in mc. and give each instance a different seed to decorrelate the noise.
The eight tap.808 voices share one accent-bus contract; the family overview patcher (tap.808.maxhelp) demonstrates them together on a running pattern.

inlet.digest: Trigger: a rising edge fires the voice; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent

method.name: signal
method.description: A rising edge triggers the voice, sample-accurately; the value the signal rises to (0..1) is the accent.

method.name: bang
method.description: Trigger the voice at full accent (from the scheduler).

method.name: trigger
method.description: Trigger the voice with an accent value (0..1, default 1).

attribute.name: model
attribute.description: The hardware's CP/MA switch: clap (default) or maracas.

attribute.name: tail
attribute.description: Level of the clap's longer noise wash, 0..2 (default 1 = stock). 0 disconnects it, leaving only the three teeth; above 1 exaggerates the wash.

attribute.name: seed
attribute.description: Seed for the noise source (default 1). Renders are bit-reproducible per seed; give mc. instances different seeds to decorrelate.
